---
title: "CodeBook"
author: "Me"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

# Code Book

## Variable Naming Convention

Each variable name follows this structure:

domain + signal + statistic + axis

Where:

- domain:  
  - time = time domain signal  
  - frequency = frequency domain signal  

- signal:  
  - body_acceleration  
  - gravity_acceleration  
  - body_gyroscopic  

- statistic:  
  - mean = average of the signal over the time window  
  - std = standard deviation of the signal  

- axis:  
  - x, y, z (spatial axes)

Example:

time_body_acceleration_mean_x

## Activity Labels

The dataset includes 30 subjects performing 6 activities:

| Class | Activity |
|------|---------|
| 1 | WALKING |
| 2 | WALKING_UPSTAIRS |
| 3 | WALKING_DOWNSTAIRS |
| 4 | SITTING |
| 5 | STANDING |
| 6 | LAYING |

## Final Dataset

The cleaned dataset includes:

- subject: ID of the subject (1–30)
- activity: activity performed
- measurement variables: mean and standard deviation of signals

## Summary Dataset

The file `measurementSummary.txt` contains the average of each variable for each subject and each activity. Each row represents a subject–activity pair.
